etymology.description: semantic 食 (food) + phonetic 反 -> meal
etymology.interpretation: 食(食) (shí, "food") provides the semantic element indicating the character's relation to food, while 反(反) (fǎn, "return") provides the sound. The association of 食 with the sound of 反 conveys the concept of 'meal' or 'food to return to'.
etymology.simplification: Simplified by replacing 食(食) (shí, "food") with 饣(饣), a reduced form.

examples.chinese: 每天我都和家人一起吃饭(每天我都和家人一起吃飯)。
examples.pinyin: Měitiān wǒ dōu hé jiārén yīqǐ chī fàn.
examples.english: Every day, I eat a meal with my family.

examples.chinese: 他做的晚餐很美味(他做的晚餐很美味)，我吃了两碗饭(我吃了兩碗飯)。
examples.pinyin: Tā zuò de wǎncān hěn měiwèi, wǒ chīle liǎng wǎn fàn.
examples.english: The dinner he made was very delicious, I ate two bowls of rice.

examples.chinese: 饭后我们去散步(飯後我們去散步)。
examples.english: We go for a walk after the meal.
